info.title: Spiffy API
info.description:
# Getting Started

The Spiffy API allows you to programmatically manage customers, orders, subscriptions, and more.

## Authentication

The Spiffy API supports two authentication methods. Which one you use depends on the type of integration you're building.

| | API Key | OAuth 2.0 |
|---|---|---|
| **For** | Private integrations | Third-party integrations |
| **Access scope** | Your account only | Any merchant who authorizes your app |
| **Setup** | Copy key from dashboard | Register OAuth client on Developer Platform |

### API Key

API key authentication is the simplest way to access the Spiffy API. It's intended for private integrations — internal tools and scripts that connect to your own Spiffy account.

Your API key is available in your Spiffy dashboard under **Settings → API**.

Pass your API key as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header on every request:

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

**Security:**
- Treat your API key like a password. Do not commit it to version control or expose it in client-side code.
- If a key is compromised, regenerate it immediately from Settings → API. The old key is invalidated instantly.

### OAuth 2.0

OAuth 2.0 is required for third-party integrations — apps that connect to other merchants' Spiffy accounts on their behalf.

**Step 1 — Redirect the merchant to authorize:**

```
https://app.spiffy.co/oauth/authorize
  ?client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID
  &redirect_uri=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI
  &response_type=code
  &scope=REQUESTED_SCOPES
  &state=RANDOM_STATE_VALUE
```

The `state` parameter should be a random, unguessable value to prevent CSRF attacks.

**Step 2 — Exchange the authorization code for tokens:**

```
POST https://api.spiffy.co/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=authorization_code
&code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE
&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID
&client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
&redirect_uri=YOUR_REDIRECT_URI
```

Response:
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600
}
```

**Step 3 — Use the access token:**

```
Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN
```

**Step 4 — Refresh when expired:**

Access tokens expire after 1 hour. Refresh tokens are valid for 1 month and are single-use — each refresh issues a new pair.

```
POST https://api.spiffy.co/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=refresh_token
&refresh_token=REFRESH_TOKEN
&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID
&client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
```

**Available scopes:** account, users, customers, checkouts, subscriptions, subscription_metered_billing, integrations, orders, payments, paymentplans, webhooks

**Token security:**
- Store tokens securely — treat them like passwords
- Never expose tokens in client-side code, URLs, or logs
- Merchants can revoke access at any time; handle 401 responses gracefully

## Rate Limiting

API requests are subject to two types of rate limiting:

### Per-Minute Rate Limit

- **100 requests per 60-second window** (fixed window)
- Applies to API key requests only (OAuth developer apps are not rate-limited per-minute)
- Response headers on every request:
  - `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum requests allowed (100)
  - `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Requests remaining in current window
  - `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Unix timestamp when the window resets

### Monthly Request Quota

Monthly limits are based on your Spiffy subscription plan:

| Plan | Monthly Limit |
|---|---|
| Pro | 10,000 |
| Scale | 50,000 |
| High Volume | 200,000 |

- Response headers on every request:
  - `X-Monthly-RateLimit-Limit`: Your monthly quota
  - `X-Monthly-RateLimit-Remaining`: Requests remaining this month
  - `X-Monthly-RateLimit-Reset`: Unix timestamp for the first of next month (UTC)
- An email warning is sent when usage reaches 80% of the monthly limit.

When either limit is exceeded, the API returns **429 Too Many Requests**:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "rate_limit_exceeded",
    "message": "Too many requests"
  }
}
```

## Pagination

List endpoints return paginated results to improve performance. You control pagination using query parameters:

| Parameter | Default | Maximum | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `page` | 1 | - | Page number (1-based) |
| `per_page` | 50 | 100 | Items per page |

**Example request:**
```
GET /v2/customers?page=2&per_page=25
```

**Response format:**
```json
{
  "data": [
    { "id": "cus_123", "email": "john@example.com", ... },
    { "id": "cus_124", "email": "jane@example.com", ... }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 2,
    "limit": 25,
    "total": 150,
    "pages": 6
  }
}
```

The `pagination` object provides:
- `page`: Current page number
- `limit`: Items per page (same as `per_page` parameter)
- `total`: Total number of items across all pages
- `pages`: Total number of pages

## Filtering

Most list endpoints support filtering to narrow results. Filters use a dot notation syntax for operators:

| Syntax | Operator | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `?filter[field]=value` | Exact match | `?filter[status]=active` |
| `?filter[field.contains]=value` | Contains (case-insensitive) | `?filter[email.contains]=gmail` |
| `?filter[field.gt]=value` | Greater than | `?filter[amount.gt]=100` |
| `?filter[field.gte]=value` | Greater than or equal | `?filter[created_at.gte]=2025-01-01` |
| `?filter[field.lt]=value` | Less than | `?filter[amount.lt]=500` |
| `?filter[field.lte]=value` | Less than or equal | `?filter[updated_at.lte]=2025-12-31` |
| `?filter[field.in]=value1,value2` | In list | `?filter[status.in]=active,trialing` |
| `?filter[field.nin]=value1,value2` | Not in list | `?filter[status.nin]=canceled,expired` |

**Complex example:**
```
GET /v2/customers?filter[email.contains]=john&filter[created_at.gte]=2025-01-01&filter[status]=active
```

This finds active customers with "john" in their email address created after January 1st, 2025.

## Searching

List endpoints support full-text search using the `search` parameter. Search is case-insensitive and works across multiple text fields relevant to each resource type.

**Example:**
```
GET /v2/customers?search=smith
```

This searches across customer names, emails, company names, and other text fields for the term "smith".

Search can be combined with filters and pagination:
```
GET /v2/customers?search=smith&filter[status]=active&page=2
```

## Resource Expansion

Many endpoints support expanding related resources in a single request using the `include` parameter. This reduces the number of API calls needed to fetch related data.

**Example:**
```
GET /v2/customers/cus_123?include=cards,stats,fields
```

This returns the customer with their payment cards, statistics, and custom fields included:

```json
{
  "id": "cus_123",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "name": "John Smith",
  // ... other customer fields ...
  "cards": [
    { "id": "card_456", "last4": "4242", "brand": "Visa" }
  ],
  "stats": {
    "total_orders": 15,
    "total_spent": 1250.00,
    "avg_order_value": 83.33
  },
  "fields": [
    { "name": "preferred_contact", "value": "email" }
  ]
}
```

Available expansions are listed in each endpoint's documentation. Multiple expansions can be requested as a comma-separated list.

## Errors

The API uses standard HTTP status codes and returns detailed error information in a consistent JSON format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "error_code",
    "message": "Human-readable error message",
    "details": {}  // Optional field-specific errors
  }
}
```

### Error Codes

| Status | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `bad_request` | The request was invalid or malformed |
| 401 | `unauthorized` | Authentication is required or has failed |
| 403 | `forbidden` | You do not have permission to access this resource |
| 403 | `invalid_scope` | Missing required OAuth scope |
| 404 | `not_found` | The requested resource does not exist |
| 409 | `conflict` | The request conflicts with the current state (e.g., duplicate email) |
| 422 | `validation_error` | The request data failed validation |
| 429 | `rate_limit_exceeded` | Too many requests |
| 500 | `internal_error` | An unexpected server error occurred |

### Validation Errors

Validation errors include additional details about which fields failed:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "validation_error",
    "message": "Validation failed",
    "details": {
      "fieldErrors": {
        "email": ["Invalid email format"],
        "name": ["String must contain at least 1 character(s)"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

tags.name: Affiliates
tags.description: Manage affiliates, affiliate programs, and payouts.

tags.name: affiliate-programs
tags.description: Affiliate program management (programs, checkouts, prices, options, links)

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Manage webhook endpoints, view event delivery logs, and browse available event types.

tags.name: API v1
tags.description: Legacy API v1 endpoints. These endpoints are stable but will not receive new features.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Account API key authentication. Pass your API key as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header. Grants full access to all endpoints — equivalent to all OAuth scopes.

OAuth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication for third-party integrations. Access is limited to the scopes granted by the merchant during authorization.

scopes.webhooks: Manage webhook endpoints and view event logs
scopes.affiliates: Read and manage affiliates and programs

customer_id.description: ID of the customer who owns this card

is_active.description: Whether the card is active and can be used for payments

is_three_d_secure_supported.description: Whether the card supports 3D Secure authentication

last_four.description: Last four digits of the card number

fingerprint.description: Unique card fingerprint for duplicate detection

updated_at.description: Timestamp when card was last updated

is_in_use.description: Whether card is used by active subscriptions or payment plans

active_subscription_count.description: Number of active subscriptions using this card

active_paymentplan_count.description: Number of active payment plans using this card

gateway_id.description: Gateway ID from the latest card source

stripe_paymentmethod_id.description: Stripe PaymentMethod ID from the latest card source

stripe_customer_id.description: Stripe Customer ID from the latest card source

paypal_billing_agreement_id.description: PayPal billing agreement ID from the latest card source

paypal_payer_id.description: PayPal payer ID from the latest card source

paypal_customer_id.description: PayPal customer ID from the latest card source

paypal_token_id.description: PayPal token ID from the latest card source

is_incompatible.description: Whether this card is incompatible with the subscription due to different Stripe customer ID

unpaid_subscription_count.description: Number of subscriptions with unpaid invoices

account_id.description: Account ID this customer belongs to

updated_at.description: Timestamp when customer was last updated

message.example: Email is already in use by another customer

user_id.description: Admin user who created order (if manual)

published_block_item_ids.description: Array of published block item IDs

detail.description: Calculated order summary (subtotal, tax, total, etc.)

url_slug.description: URL slug for order completion page

is_commissionable.description: Whether this item generates affiliate commission

is_refunded_by_spiffy.description: Whether refund is handled by Spiffy

payment_ids.description: Array of payment IDs for this item

customer_id.description: Customer who made the payment (sourced from the parent order; included for convenience so customer-centric reporting doesn't require a second hop through /v2/orders)

is_3ds_in_progress.description: Whether 3D Secure authentication is in progress

is_startup_fee.description: Whether this is a startup/setup fee

customer_id.description: Customer ID who owns this source

is_trial_end_upcoming_sent.description: Whether trial end notification was sent

payment_ids.description: Array of payment IDs (excluding initial payment)

retry_schedule.description: Pending retry schedule for past_due subscriptions (array of date strings)

price.description: Product option price with nested option and product

card.description: Payment card associated with this subscription

gateway.description: Payment gateway used for this subscription

discount_type.description: Type of discount (fixed amount or percentage)

discount_offset.description: Discount value (cents for amount, percentage for percent)

item_ids.description: Order item IDs in this plan

retry_schedule.description: Pending retry schedule for past_due plans (array of date strings)

events.description: List of event types to subscribe to (defaults to ['*'])
List of event types to subscribe to
